claim: OB archiver stores grow without bound because no GC action exists for ob_archiver_store (asymmetry with CE archivers which have two GC paths). Adding a session-scoped OB archiver GC action that removes effects from sessions older than ob_primary[m] is safe because all consumers (OBViewChange, OMSViewChange) query OBArchiverSeqsForSession with the current session only, never old sessions. This eliminates unbounded state growth in OB archivers without weakening any property.
preconditions: ob_archiver_store grows monotonically (no action removes from it in the original spec, lines 1013-1014)
OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, session) filters by session field (line 211), so old-session data is invisible to current-session queries

OBViewChange (line 428-453) uses OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, ob_primary[m]) where ob_primary[m] is the OLD session at read time (before increment). After view change completes, ob_primary advances and old-session data becomes unreachable
OMSViewChange (line 518) uses OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, ob_primary[m]) with the CURRENT ob_primary. It never queries sessions older than current
ob_archiver_pending is NOT touched by this proposal. CE still reads ob_archiver_pending via CEProcessRecoveredPending (line 327-345). Pending data GC is a separate concern
structural_delta: One new action (ArchiverGCOBBySession). INV20 comment updated to reflect OB archiver GC exists. No new variables. No fairness conjuncts added (GC is not liveness-critical, fires when enabled). State reduction: |OBArchivers| * accumulated_old_session_effects records removed from ob_archiver_store after each OB view change.
rationale: The original spec (line 1013) states 'OB archivers: effects never removed (no GC actions touch ob_archiver_store)' and calls this structural. However, this creates asymmetric behavior between CE and OB archivers. CE archivers have two GC mechanisms: ArchiverGCByAcks (backup watermark, modified in step 1) and ArchiverGCByCheckpoint (checkpoint endorsement). OB archivers have zero. In any long-running execution with OB view changes, ob_archiver_store accumulates effects from all historical sessions that will never be queried again. The session field in OB archiver records makes old-session data identifiable and safely removable. The GC guard (e.session < ob_primary[e.market]) ensures only old-session effects are removed. Current-session effects are preserved for OBViewChange recovery and OMSViewChange catch-up. The guard also checks ob_alive[e.market] to prevent GC during OB downtime when ob_primary still reflects the crashed session. Idempotency: the action checks that at least one old-session effect exists before firing, preventing spurious re-firing on already-clean stores.

diff.description: Add ArchiverGCOBBySession action, update Next, update INV20 comment. All changes against ORIGINAL spec.

hunks.location: After ArchiverGCByCheckpoint (after line 715), before Message Loss section
new_lines: \* Archiver GC for OB: remove effects from sessions older than current OB primary
\* Safe because OBArchiverSeqsForSession always queries current session.
\* ob_archiver_pending is NOT touched (CE may still need pending data).
/\ \E e \in ob_archiver_store[a] : e.market = m /\ e.session < ob_primary[m]

hunks.location: Next state relation, OB archiver section (after line 811)

old_lines: \* OB archivers: effects never removed (no GC actions touch ob_archiver_store)
\* this is structural (no action modifies ob_archiver_store except ingest)
\* but stated explicitly as a checkable invariant
new_lines: \* OB archivers: only old-session effects removed (ArchiverGCOBBySession)
\* current-session effects preserved for OBViewChange and OMSViewChange

property_analysis.P7_ArchiverDedup: Unaffected. P7 is about CE archivers only.
property_analysis.INV20_ArchiverDAMonotonicity: Revised. Old comment claimed OB effects never removed. New INV20 checks that all OB archiver effects have session >= ob_primary[e.market] (current session). The GC action only removes effects with session < ob_primary[m], so surviving effects satisfy the invariant. After OBViewChange increments ob_primary, old-session effects become GC-eligible.
property_analysis.P1_TypeInvariant: Unaffected. ob_archiver_store remains a set of records with same type.
property_analysis.P18_OMSMutedUntilCaughtUp: Unaffected. Checks oms_ob_cursor vs ob_seq, not archiver contents.

safety_argument: The GC action removes effects where e.session < ob_primary[m]. The only consumers of ob_archiver_store are: (1) OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, session) called by OBViewChange with session = ob_primary[m] (the current session at call time, before increment). At the moment OBViewChange fires, ob_primary[m] is the old session S. OBViewChange reads OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, S), then increments to S+1. The GC action requires ob_alive[m], which is FALSE when OBViewChange fires (~ob_alive[m] guard on line 429). So GC cannot fire during view change. After view change completes, ob_alive[m]=TRUE and ob_primary[m]=S+1. GC can now remove session S effects. This is safe because nobody will ever query session S again. (2) OMSViewChange uses OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, ob_primary[m]) with the CURRENT session. After OB view change to S+1, OMS will query session S+1 only. OMS does not need session S data from OB archivers.
liveness_argument: No fairness conjunct added for ArchiverGCOBBySession. GC is not liveness-critical: it reduces state but no property depends on it eventually firing. The action is enabled whenever ob_alive[m] and old-session effects exist. It fires nondeterministically when enabled. No existing fairness guarantees are weakened because no existing fairness conjunct references ob_archiver_store.
